MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING JURY TRIAL PURSUANT TO [CODE OF CIV. PROC. 631(4)] OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE RELIEF FROM WAIVER OF TRIAL BY JURY [CODE CIV. PROC. 631(E)] (TRANSACTION ID # 100229468) FILED BY PLAINTIFF BYNUM, SHANEE HEARING SET FOR MAR-20-2024 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 206

MASTER MOTION CALENDAR ON MAR-20-2024 IN DEPT. 206, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING JURY TRIAL PURSUANT TO [CODE OF CIV. PROC. 631(4)] OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE RELIEF FROM WAIVER OF TRIAL BY JURY [CODE CIV. PROC. 631(E)]. BRANDON BANKS, ESQ. APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. KAYLA MALONE, ESQ. AND LOWELL RITTER, ESQ. APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT ST. ANTHONY FOUNDATION VIA COURTCALL. COURT MAKES A DISCLOSURE THAT HE KNOWS MR. BANKS FROM THE P.D.'S OFFICE AND MOTHER FROM TRIPLE A. HE NEVER HAD ANY SOCIAL ACTIVITIES WITH THEM. DEFENDANT HAS NO OBJECTION AND WOULD LIKE TO PROCEED WITH THE HEARING. AFTER ARGUMENT, THE COURT DOES NOT FIND ANY PREJUDICE IN SWITCHING GEARS IN PIVOTING FROM A COURT TRIAL TO JURY TRIAL. THE COURT GRANTS PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM WAIVER OF JURY TRIAL BASED ON THE FOLLOWING: (1) NO PREJUDICE TO DEFENSE AND (2) THE NON-FILING WAS INADVERTENT. IF PARTIES NEED MORE TIME TO CONTINUE THE TRIAL, PARTIES MAY STIPULATE TO CONTINUE THE TRIAL OR FILE A NOTICED MOTION AND INCLUDE IN THE DECLARATION THAT THE COURT HAS GRANTED RELIEF FROM WAIVER OF JURY TRIAL. THE TRIAL DATE OF MAY 6, 2024 IS SET AS A JURY TRIAL. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER THAT IS APPROVED AS TO FORM AND CONFORMS WITH THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: SAMUEL K. FENG; CLERK: R. VELUZ; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)
